companion_files.name: HANDOFF.md
companion_files.content:
# LLM Session Handoff (2026-02-12)

## Branch baseline vs `main`
- Current branch: `feature/listen-together-pass-3`
- Merge-base with `main`: `4be7852386cbee2f38f95f998c31dea908232400`
- Divergence: `0 behind / 13 ahead` (same result vs `origin/main`)
- Diff summary (`main...HEAD`): `39 files changed, +5149 / -144`
- Top-level scope by file count:
  - `frontend`: 21
  - `backend`: 10
  - `charts`: 3
  - `docs`: 2
  - root files: `.gitignore`, `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`

## Commits since branch diverged from `main`
(Oldest -> newest)
1. `2304c81` (2026-02-11) `oneshot...`
2. `ab869d8` (2026-02-11) `few bugs; lots of ui issues`
3. `96c595d` (2026-02-11) `doc updates, lint fixes`
4. `cde18f6` (2026-02-12) `lots of changes with how groups function`
5. `247d7b8` (2026-02-12) `frontend timeout simplification`
6. `d813b34` (2026-02-12) `ui for buttons fix`
7. `9bbde50` (2026-02-12) `attempt to address some stalling when immediately interacting with media during playback`
8. `fdc1d63` (2026-02-12) `playback performance optimizations`
9. `c079b50` (2026-02-12) `frontend enhancements`
10. `8a5ae87` (2026-02-12) `frontend docs for socket.io and more`
11. `9d6e6c1` (2026-02-12) `frontend docs for socket.io and more`

## High-level delta themes vs `main`
1. Listen Together backend feature work
- Added Sync Group schema/migrations and backend routes/services/socket orchestration.
- Key files: `backend/prisma/schema.prisma`, `backend/src/routes/listenTogether.ts`, `backend/src/services/listenTogether*.ts`, `backend/src/index.ts`.

2. Listen Together frontend integration + UI behavior
- New/updated context/socket/session handling and Listen Together pages/components.
- Queue/session behavior and sidebar/session indicators touched.
- Key files: `frontend/lib/listen-together-*.ts*`, `frontend/app/listen-together/page.tsx`, `frontend/app/queue/page.tsx`, `frontend/components/layout/{Sidebar,MobileSidebar}.tsx`, `frontend/components/player/SyncBadge.tsx`.

3. Playback responsiveness and API failure behavior
- Timeout/retry and proxy error shaping updates.
- Playback control hot-path/perf-related changes.
- Key files: `frontend/lib/api.ts`, `frontend/lib/apiProxy.ts`, `frontend/lib/audio-controls-context.tsx`.

4. Player and page UX adjustments
- Standard player updates (mini/full/overlay), radio page change, queue polish.
- Key files: `frontend/components/player/{MiniPlayer,FullPlayer,OverlayPlayer}.tsx`, `frontend/app/radio/page.tsx`.

5. Deployment/ops and docs
- Helm routing/docs updates for Listen Together Socket.IO pathing.
- Key files: `charts/lidify/templates/{ingress,gateway-httproute}.yaml`, `charts/lidify/README.md`, `README.md`, `docs/FORK_DIFF.md`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `docs/REVERSE_PROXY_AND_TUNNELS.md`.

## Specific work completed in this session
- Investigated Listen Together stuck `Connecting...` in k8s `deploymentMode: individual`.
- Confirmed Socket.IO client/server settings:
  - namespace `/listen-together`
  - path `/socket.io/listen-together`
- Added explicit path routing to backend (`:3006`) in Helm ingress and gateway templates.
- Added reverse-proxy + Cloudflare Tunnel setup guidance and linked it from root/chart READMEs.
- Documented env-var context split (`BACKEND_URL` vs `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` vs `127.0.0.1`) in proxy/tunnel doc.

## Follow-up findings (same day)
- Cloudflare Tunnel `ingress.path` matching is regex-based (not glob-based), so `path: /socket.io/listen-together*` can fail to match Socket.IO handshake requests that include a trailing slash.
- Updated tunnel example to `path: /socket.io/listen-together.*`.
- Added direct probe guidance:
  - test URL: `/socket.io/listen-together/?EIO=4&transport=polling`
  - expected result: Socket.IO JSON response, not frontend HTML 404 page.
- Practical implication: opening bare `/socket.io/listen-together` in a browser is not a definitive websocket test by itself.

## Listen Together fixes added after follow-up
- Fixed an LT sync echo path where remote state application could trigger duplicate host Socket.IO commands (play/pause/seek), which could intermittently cause extra skips/advances.
- Added sync-only control options in frontend audio controls so LT remote sync can:
  - seek followers without being blocked by host-only local seek guard
  - update local playback without re-broadcasting to server.
- Updated LT socket-state handling:
  - force-apply only on initial/reconnect snapshot, not every `group:state`
  - apply `play-at` for all members (including host) using suppressed local controls.
- Added waiting readiness tolerance:
  - waits briefly for expected track to appear locally (or timeout) before `ready` ack.
- Added real-time member presence transitions from backend:
  - broadcasts `group:state` when member socket connectivity changes
  - re-checks ready gate when a waiting member disconnects.
- Fixed backend stability issue in Socket.IO no-payload events:
  - `ready`, `leave-group`, and `lt-ping` handlers now resolve `ack` defensively whether client emits callback-only or payload+callback.
  - prevents `TypeError: ack is not a function` crash loops seen on next-track/auto-advance transitions.

## Validation performed
1. Render checks:
- `helm template lidify charts/lidify --set deploymentMode=individual --set ingress.enabled=true`
- `helm template lidify charts/lidify --set deploymentMode=individual --set gateway.enabled=true`
- Verified `/socket.io/listen-together` routes to backend `:3006`.

2. Lint:
- `helm lint charts/lidify`
- Result: pass.

## Current repo state
- Working tree: clean (`git status -sb`)
- `LLM_SESSION_HANDOFF.md` is intentionally ignored by git via `.gitignore` pattern `*_HANDOFF.md`.

## Next checks for next agent/operator
1. Deploy updated chart/templates to cluster.
2. For Cloudflare Tunnel, ensure the listen-together rule uses regex prefix semantics:
   - `path: /socket.io/listen-together.*`
   - placed before the frontend catch-all rule.
3. Validate websocket upgrade for `/socket.io/listen-together` in browser devtools.
4. Confirm Listen Together state transitions to connected (no persistent `Connecting...`).
5. If still failing, inspect proxy/gateway websocket timeout and upgrade settings end-to-end.

## Added in this session (routing gate + operator guidance)
- Listen Together frontend now runs a socket route preflight against:
  - `/socket.io/listen-together/?EIO=4&transport=polling`
- When preflight fails (frontend HTML, timeout, network error, or wrong upstream), UI now:
  - shows an explicit routing error banner
  - disables create/join actions in the lobby
  - exposes a manual "Re-check Socket Route" action
  - shows "Routing required" in active-session connection status when misconfigured
- Create/join REST actions and initial socket connect are now gated on successful route probe.
- Docs were updated to emphasize this requirement applies to all split deployments (not just Helm):
  - `docs/REVERSE_PROXY_AND_TUNNELS.md`
  - `README.md`

companion_files.name: PLAN.md
companion_files.content:
# Listen Together Plan (One-Shot from `main`)

This file is a standalone implementation blueprint for building Listen Together from a clean `main` branch.
It includes architecture, contracts, UX rules, deployment requirements, and validation criteria based on:

- `AGENTS.md`
- `CHANGELOG.md` (`1.5.0` entries)
- `PROMPT_HISTORY.md` (Listen Together intent)
- `LLM_SESSION_HANDOFF.md` (latest branch state)
- branch comparison: `main...feature/listen-together-pass-3` (`0 behind / 28 ahead`, `58 files changed`, `+6336/-329`)

## 1. Product Scope

Listen Together is a real-time synchronized listening feature for authenticated users.

Core behavior:

1. Users create or join groups.
2. Group has one shared, server-authoritative queue.
3. Playback synchronization is real-time via Socket.IO.
4. Transport control is host-only (host-follower mode).
5. All members can edit queue (add/remove/clear); reordering is disabled.
6. Only local library tracks are eligible for group queue.
7. Group state survives backend restarts via periodic DB persistence.

Final scope intentionally does not include collaborative transport mode.

## 2. Non-Negotiable Rules

1. Real-time path must be Socket.IO, not SSE.
2. Hot-path operations must be in-memory (no DB writes on every play/pause/seek).
3. Monotonic `stateVersion` must gate client state application to prevent stale packet visual rewinds.
4. Socket route preflight must gate create/join to avoid permanent `Connecting...` when reverse proxy is misrouted.
5. Listen Together queue accepts only local tracks (no TIDAL, YouTube, preview-only, podcast, audiobook).
6. Leaving/ending group must optimistically unblock UI before REST completes.
7. Host transfer is automatic when host leaves (alphabetical username, then join order).

## 3. Data Model and Migrations

Add Prisma models and relations:

1. `SyncGroup`
2. `SyncGroupMember`
3. `User.syncGroupsHosted`
4. `User.syncGroupMemberships`
5. `Track.syncGroups`

Required `SyncGroup` fields:

1. `id`, `name`, `joinCode`, `groupType`, `visibility`, `hostUserId`, `isActive`
2. `trackId`, `queue` (`Json`), `currentIndex`, `isPlaying`, `currentTime`
3. `stateVersion`, `stateUpdatedAt`, `endedAt`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`

Required `SyncGroupMember` fields:

1. `id`, `syncGroupId`, `userId`, `isHost`, `joinedAt`, `lastSeenAt`, `leftAt`

Migration sequence:

1. Create base tables/indexes/FKs.
2. Add enhancement columns (`groupType`, `visibility`, `stateVersion`) and visibility-aware index.

## 4. Backend Implementation

### 4.1 New backend files

1. `backend/src/routes/listenTogether.ts`
2. `backend/src/services/listenTogether.ts`
3. `backend/src/services/listenTogetherManager.ts`
4. `backend/src/services/listenTogetherSocket.ts`

### 4.2 Route mounting and lifecycle

In `backend/src/index.ts`:

1. Mount REST router at `/api/listen-together`.
2. Create HTTP server and attach Socket.IO namespace setup.
3. Start persistence loop on boot.
4. On graceful shutdown:
   - stop persistence loop
   - persist all groups
   - close listen-together socket server

### 4.3 REST cold-path contracts

Implement and validate:

1. `POST /listen-together` create group
2. `POST /listen-together/join` join by code
3. `GET /listen-together/discover` discover public groups
4. `GET /listen-together/active-count` active session count
5. `GET /listen-together/mine` current user active group
6. `POST /listen-together/:groupId/leave` leave group
7. `POST /listen-together/:groupId/end` optional host hard-end route (not primary UI flow)

Notes:

1. Auto-leave existing active group before create/join.
2. Validate and normalize join code.
3. Convert incoming queue IDs to validated local-track queue items only.
4. Use typed error mapping (`GroupError` -> 4xx) and Zod 400s.

### 4.4 In-memory hot-path manager

`listenTogetherManager.ts` must own:

1. Group state map
2. Member state map (socket IDs, readiness, connected/disconnected)
3. Playback state (queue/index/play/pause/position/version)
4. Ready gate orchestration for track changes
5. Callback bridge to socket broadcaster

Required operations:

1. `play`, `pause`, `seek`
2. `setTrack`, `next`, `previous`
3. `modifyQueue(add/remove/clear, reject reorder)`
4. `reportReady`
5. `addMember/removeMember`
6. host transfer
7. auto-disband if group empty
8. snapshot + deltas

Ready gate behavior:

1. On track change with multiple connected members:
   - move to `waiting`
   - emit `group:waiting`
   - collect `ready`
   - force start at timeout (`8s`)
2. Emit `group:play-at` when gate opens.

### 4.5 Socket namespace

Use namespace `/listen-together` and path `/socket.io/listen-together`.

Handshake auth:

1. JWT required in `socket.handshake.auth.token`
2. verify token and user existence
3. enforce tokenVersion if provided

Client -> server events:

1. `join-group`
2. `playback` (`play|pause|seek|next|previous|set-track`)
3. `queue` (`add|remove|reorder|clear`)
4. `ready`
5. `lt-ping`
6. `leave-group`

Server -> client events:

1. `group:state`
2. `group:playback-delta`
3. `group:queue-delta`
4. `group:waiting`
5. `group:play-at`
6. `group:member-joined`
7. `group:member-left`
8. `group:ended`

Ack handling hardening:

1. support `ack-only` and `payload + ack` for no-payload events
2. guard `ack` type before calling to prevent `TypeError: ack is not a function`

### 4.6 Persistence strategy

`listenTogether.ts` should:

1. persist dirty groups every `30s`
2. persist on shutdown
3. hydrate group from DB if in-memory state missing
4. prefer in-memory state for discovery/mine responses when present

## 5. Frontend Implementation

### 5.1 New frontend files

1. `frontend/app/listen-together/page.tsx`
2. `frontend/lib/listen-together-socket.ts`
3. `frontend/lib/listen-together-context.tsx`
4. `frontend/lib/listen-together-session.ts`
5. `frontend/hooks/useActiveListenSessions.ts`
6. `frontend/hooks/useQueuedTrackIds.ts`
7. `frontend/components/player/SyncBadge.tsx`
8. `frontend/components/ui/EqBars.tsx`

### 5.2 API client additions

In `frontend/lib/api.ts`, add Listen Together REST methods matching backend routes.

### 5.3 Provider wiring

Wrap authenticated app tree with `ListenTogetherProvider` inside existing audio provider stack:

1. `AudioStateProvider`
2. `AudioPlaybackProvider`
3. `AudioControlsProvider`
4. `ListenTogetherProvider`

### 5.4 Socket client + route preflight

`listen-together-socket.ts` responsibilities:

1. manage singleton Socket.IO connection lifecycle
2. typed event handlers/emit wrappers
3. route preflight check against:
   - `/socket.io/listen-together/?EIO=4&transport=polling`
4. cache probe results briefly
5. classify probe failures (`frontend-route`, `http-error`, `timeout`, `network-error`, `unexpected-response`)

### 5.5 ListenTogether context responsibilities

`listen-together-context.tsx` must:

1. fetch `mine` on startup
2. connect/reconnect socket when active group exists
3. bridge server state to audio controls/state
4. enforce `stateVersion` gating on snapshot and deltas
5. suppress rebroadcast when applying remote playback locally
6. gate create/join on successful route probe
7. expose `recheckSocketRoute` action and UI-facing route status flags
8. persist lightweight session snapshot to localStorage

### 5.6 Group page UX

`/listen-together` page needs:

Lobby:

1. create form (name, public/private, use current queue toggle)
2. join by code
3. discover public groups with periodic refresh
4. socket-route error banner + recheck action
5. disable create/join while route invalid/checking

Active view:

1. group header, join code copy, connection status
2. compact now-playing text
3. member list with host and connected indicators
4. queue list with host-only play-now
5. clear/remove actions
6. leave session action only (host handoff/last-member cleanup handles lifecycle)

### 5.7 Global playback control integration

`frontend/lib/audio-controls-context.tsx` must integrate Listen Together behavior:

1. if active LT session, browse-view play actions should queue into group instead of force switching playback
2. host-only transport broadcasts for play/pause/seek/next/previous/set-track
3. follower resume aligns to host server position
4. follower local seek blocked unless explicitly allowed by remote-sync path
5. preserve existing non-group behavior

### 5.8 Queue semantics and eligibility rules

Implement across artist/album/library/popular/playlist/mix/search/discover:

1. local-only enforcement for LT add/play paths
2. explicit user-facing errors for blocked source types
3. red lock-state action bars in LT for disabled controls (play/shuffle/download)
4. `Add All to Queue` affordances where requested
5. popular-track click in LT should queue only clicked track
6. artist radio and vibe in LT should queue full returned set and require confirmation
7. when host creates group from current queue, preserve current track/time/play-state when possible

### 5.9 Queue visibility indicators

Use shared hook (`useQueuedTrackIds`) so track-list UIs display subtle queued-state highlight and `IN QUEUE` badge using:

1. group queue when in LT
2. personal queue otherwise

Apply to album, artist popular, library tracks, discover tracks, search library tracks, playlist, mix.

### 5.10 Player and navigation indicators

1. Add `SyncBadge` in mini/full player while in group.
2. Sidebar/mobile nav should show active-session indicator (green border + EQ bars) via periodic active-count polling.

## 6. Deployment and Proxy Requirements

For split frontend/backend deployments:

1. route `/socket.io/listen-together` to backend `:3006`
2. route app pages to frontend `:3030`

Helm templates must include explicit LT socket path rule in individual mode:

1. `charts/lidify/templates/ingress.yaml`
2. `charts/lidify/templates/gateway-httproute.yaml`

Document proxy/tunnel setup and probe guidance in:

1. `docs/REVERSE_PROXY_AND_TUNNELS.md`
2. `README.md`
3. `charts/lidify/README.md`

Cloudflare Tunnel note:

1. path matcher is regex; use `/socket.io/listen-together.*`

## 7. File-Level Delivery Checklist

Backend:

1. Prisma schema + migrations for sync group models
2. listen-together REST routes
3. in-memory manager with ready gate + host transfer + queue mutation
4. socket namespace and authenticated event handlers
5. startup/shutdown persistence wiring

Frontend:

1. API methods
2. socket wrapper + probe
3. LT provider/context
4. LT page (lobby + active states)
5. audio controls integration and remote apply safeguards
6. queue highlights and LT UI lock/eligibility rules across views
7. sidebar/mobile/player badges and indicators

Docs:

1. `CHANGELOG.md` (`[1.5.0] - Unreleased`)
2. `README.md`
3. `docs/FORK_DIFF.md`
4. LT proxy/tunnel doc and chart README

## 8. Validation Matrix

Run:

1. `cd backend && npm test` (or at minimum targeted tests if suite unavailable)
2. `cd frontend && npm run lint`
3. `helm lint charts/lidify` if chart changed

Manual scenarios:

1. create public/private group, join by code, discover list updates
2. host transfer on host leave
3. auto-disband when last member leaves
4. ready-gate sync on next/set-track with 2+ members
5. follower join mid-song starts near live position, not `0:00`
6. stale/equal version packets do not cause visual rewind
7. non-local track add blocked with clear error
8. browse play actions in LT append queue and do not force switch track
9. queue highlight appears in all targeted track lists
10. misrouted socket path shows routing banner and blocks create/join
11. fixed routing allows connect and removes banner

## 9. Definition of Done

Feature is complete when:

1. backend and frontend contracts match exactly
2. synchronization is stable under reconnects and host changes
3. LT source-eligibility and queue semantics match user intent from prompt history
4. split-deployment routing failure mode is explicitly detected and surfaced in UI
5. documentation and changelog entries are updated for fork process requirements

companion_files.name: PROMPT_HISTORY.md
companion_files.content:
# Prompt History

This file records user prompts by session and feature area so future work can reuse prior intent/context.

## Session: 2026-02-12

- Session recorded at (UTC): `2026-02-12T16:39:57Z`
- Timestamp model used below: `T+NN` session-relative order index.
- Note: exact per-message wall-clock timestamps are not exposed in this transcript context, so history is preserved with strict sequence order.

### Listen Together Sync + Visual State

- `T+00`  
  `"Use LLM_SESSION_HANDOFF, AGENTS.md, as well as the LISTEN_TOGETHER_PLAN.md (may be outdated based on where we are) for context. While in a listen-together group, clicking "next track" or another track on the queue, visibly it shows it skipping a track or two, but ultimately playing the next song anyway."`
- `T+01`  
  `"Let me be clear, the skip is visual. The actual song that is supposed to play does appear to play, but all visual indicators show that it's not. If your plan fixes this, then I approve"`

### Listen Together Group Interaction Rules

- `T+04`  
  `"- When in a listen-together group, the play button in the library view, or album from an artist view should not exist.
  - When in a listen-together group, in the artist view and album view, the play button, shuffle button, vibe button, and "Download all" button should be wrapped around a red border and display an error about being in a group and these not being functional.
  - When in a listen-together group, any action that essentially adds something to a queue (artist view, library view, album view, and starts playing from that spot should only add it to the group queue and NOT start playing it. We want the queue to play through unless the host explicitly changes the song with the next/previous track buttons, or from within the queue itself.
  - When I click "add to queue" from any view where it's visible, we should have a toast that tells the user it was added to the queue"`
- `T+05`  
  `"- When in a listen-together group and someone attempts to add/play a track that doesn't exist in the library (tidal, ytmusic, preview, podcast, etc) they should be warned/not allowed to add it. It should be visibly unable to be added as well
  - When someone presses the "start artist radio" or "vibe" buttons while in a group, those should add ALL of the appropriate tracks to the queue, not just the first track."`
- `T+06`  
  `"Yes, take all the action listed in your plan"`

### Queue Visibility UX

- `T+07`  
  `"When looking at a track list, whether it's popular, album view, searched songs in library (in both standard and listen-together group mode), I'd like to see a faint highlight of the track if it's currently in queue, or some visible indicator that it's in the queue already (server/group queue or individual queue)"`
- `T+08`  
  `"Feels okay to me"`
- `T+09`  
  `"Do it."`

### Workflow / Execution Directives

- `T+02`  
  `"Warning: apply_patch was requested via exec_command. Use the apply_patch tool instead of exec_command."`
- `T+03`  
  `"Warning: apply_patch was requested via exec_command. Use the apply_patch tool instead of exec_command."`
- `T+10`  
  `"commit it"`

### Process + Context Retention

- `T+11`  
  `"I also want you to record all of my prompts from this session (with timestamps/history) in a "prompt history" markdown file (separated by feature) as well at the root, as well as use them as past context."`

### Listen Together Host Group-Start Continuity

- `T+12`  
  `"If it's not already in AGENTS.md, make sure future sessions know to use LLM_SESSION_HANDOFF.md if it exists, as well"`
- `T+13`  
  `"- When I start a listen group as a host, the playback stops and the current song moves to the top of the playlist. I would like to keep playing whatever song I'm playing at the moment while still loading the whole queue into the server/group queue."`
- `T+14`  
  `"Implement"`

### Listen Together Bulk-Action Confirmation + Popular Behavior

- `T+15`  
  `"- When in a group, if "start artist radio" or "vibe" buttons are pressed, we need to warn the user that this will add many songs to the queue and ask them if they are sure they want to do it with a confirmation.

  - When in a group, if someone clicks a song in the "Popular" tab, we do not want to add the whole popular list of songs to the queue, just the one they clicked

  - Let's add a link to the "Popular" text that takes us to the popular page

  - We may have a bug in the artist/album lockout functionality.  Even though I have albums marked owned and in library, every item in an album view is showing as a "preview" item and blocked from being added to queue."`

### Listen Together Add-All Buttons

- `T+16`  
  `"- while in a group, buttons in the Artist, Popular, and Album views should have an "add all to queue""`

### Radio Stations UI Contrast

- `T+17`  
  `"- on the radio stations page, all the "play" buttons should be orange play buttons.  Right now, those buttons are dark and hard to see"`

### Listen Together Plan Refresh + Queue UX Follow-ups

- `T+18`  
  `"Use AGENTS.md, as well as any _PLAN.md file that may exist as it relates to the feature the user intends on building (may be behind if we've forgotten to write to it) for context to begin.  If no plan exists as it relates to the feature, you may create it (suffixed with _PLAN.md).

Use your knowledge of this whole project, AGENTS.md, Changelog, LLM_SESSION_HANDOFF, PROMPT_HISTORY.md, and LISTEN_TOGETHER_PLAN.md to update the listen together plan file with relevant context and goals under the assumption if I were to branch from main branch cleanly and provide an LLM with the plan file only, there would be a high chance that it would be able to oneshot the feature.

Don't forget to compare the main branch and this current branch (feature/listen-together-pass-3) if you haven't already"`
- `T+19`  
  `"\"Add all to queue\" should not be surrounded by a red box when in a listen group"`
- `T+20`  
  `"Is there a universal queue icon that we could use to place next to the "add to playlist" icon, instead of exposing a large button using the same playlist icon?

I think I would like the "add all to queue" function to also persist outside of just when in a listen-group.  This way the user can add all tracks to their queue without re-writing the entire queue and starting over (like the orange play button does)"`
- `T+21`  
  `"Does this account for the "add to queue" functionality on single tracks in album view as well?"`
- `T+22`  
  `"As the host, whenever I'm clicking a track in the "Up next/queue" view (in the overlay player), it's just adding the song to the queue again, not switching to it (as it previously was I believe).  Regression?"`
- `T+23`  
  `"Occasionally the frontend disconnects from the backend.
Tell me what this error is in the frontend and how we can handle it better:
lidify-frontend-statefulset-0 lidify ⨯ Error: failed to pipe response
    at e0 (.next/server/chunks/[root-of-the-server]__8f5ebbc3._.js:5:3508)
    at async tp (.next/server/chunks/[root-of-the-server]__8f5ebbc3._.js:5:23202)
    at async d (.next/server/chunks/[root-of-the-server]__023bb85d._.js:1:6877)
    at async l (.next/server/chunks/[root-of-the-server]__023bb85d._.js:1:7742)
    at async Module.N (.next/server/chunks/[root-of-the-server]__023bb85d._.js:1:8820) {
  [cause]: TypeError: terminated
      at ignore-listed frames {
    [cause]: Error [SocketError]: other side closed
        at ignore-listed frames {
      code: 'UND_ERR_SOCKET',
      socket: {
        localAddress: '10.244.5.221',
        localPort: 46504,
        remoteAddress: '10.99.230.8',
        remotePort: 3006,
        remoteFamily: 'IPv4',

### Listen Together Reliability + Overlay Session Indicator

- `T+30`  
  `"Use AGENTS.md, as well as any _PLAN.md file that may exist as it relates to the feature the user intends on building (may be behind if we've forgotten to write to it) for context to begin.  If no plan exists as it relates to the feature, you may create it (suffixed with _PLAN.md).

We should have a badge/icon in the overlay player to indicate that we are in a group-listen session by the way.

I feel like we have a frontend/backend disconnect, latency, or delay/slowness more often than we should.  Group play feels worse after that last commit actually where we messed with the howler audio engine.

Sometimes I get no audio, but visually it looks like it's still playing (no group mode).  A full refresh of the page and then pressing play fixes it.

When not in group-mode, skipping forward/backward works great and perfect.  When in listen-together mode however, the host clicking forward specifically will visually skip multiple tracks in the server queue.  the moment we skip forward/backwards, the client/guest becomes desynced.  I see no errors on the backend.  Their visual play bar is stuck at 0 on the right song that hsould be playing, but nothing is playing and the progress marker isn't moving.  The host moving the song around updates on the client, but the playback never happens. (Regression it feels like since the last working deployment (09354c8 I think)"`
